behavior_summary: This script implements a town guard NPC that (a) responds to being examined with a short identification bark, (b) reacts briefly when the player enters its fast trigger area by spawning short-lived animations/behaviors, (c) runs a scheduled handler that can clear a dead state (revive/reset) and spawn activation behavior, and (d) provides a multi-branch interactive dialog on Use which can interrogate the player, read world-state flags to adapt answers, move or face the NPC, and transition to combat if the player responds hostilely.

Dialog behavior reads several global flags (hydrosFree, pyrosFree, hasHeart, benticCacked, devonInRule, guardEWMet, benticMet, askedAboutEWRoad, interrogEW) to choose greetings, alarm/taunt lines, and available question lists. The interrogation branch sets interrogEW to prevent repetition. A specific player reply ('No!' / hostile reply) causes an explicit combat transition (setEnemyAlignment, setInCombat, setTarget and spawn of a combat method).

Fast-area and schedule handlers primarily spawn helper methods (METHOD::091C, METHOD::15E7, METHOD::143A, METHOD::133F) to orchestrate animations, timing, and possible revival behavior; these are spawned rather than implemented inline.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GUARD_EW::look: immediate branch on Npc::isDead(self) — dead => bark 'dead guardsman', alive => bark 'guardsman'.
U8P_GUARD_EW::enterFastArea: returns immediately if Npc::isDead(self); otherwise spawns short animation/behavior via METHOD::091C and METHOD::15E7 to react to the entering actor.
U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule: spawns METHOD::143A (timing/patrol). If spawn result indicates revival condition, it calls Npc::clrDead(self) and spawns METHOD::133F as follow-up activation.

U8P_GUARD_EW::use: spawns METHOD::0BCF to set up interaction, builds a list of question strings (slist) for Item::ask, then performs strcmp-based branching on the returned string. Branches read or update multiple global flags and may call Npc::pathfind/Npc::getDir and spawn METHOD::15E7 to make the guard move/facing changes. Hostile player input triggers enemy alignment and combat setup plus spawn of METHOD::1090.
open_questions: Exact semantics and parameter meanings of the various METHOD::* spawn helpers (METHOD::091C, METHOD::15E7, METHOD::143A, METHOD::133F, METHOD::0BCF, METHOD::1090) — their names are unknown and behavior is inferred from usage patterns.

Precise conditions returned by METHOD::143A that cause schedule() to call Npc::clrDead() (what return code corresponds to revival?), and whether schedule is also used for other timed behaviors besides revival.

Whether any side-effects beyond dialogue occur for specific branches (e.g., reputation changes, quest progression) because only reads/writes of a subset of global flags are visible in the disassembly excerpt.

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD_EW::look
main_function.purpose: Handle examine/U8P_GUARD_EW::look events: provide an immediate identification bark; say 'dead guardsman' if the NPC is dead, otherwise 'guardsman'.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (player/actor looks at this guard object)

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD_EW::look
function_map.role: Examine handler that barks a short identification line and branches on death state.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD_EW::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area trigger: when an actor enters the guard's trigger region spawn short reaction behaviors unless the guard is dead.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD_EW::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic/scheduled handler: spawn scheduling behavior, inspect result, possibly clear dead state (revive) and spawn follow-up activation.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD_EW::use
function_map.role: Main interaction handler (Use): orchestrates dialog trees, question lists, world-state dependent answers, movement/facing actions, interrogation-state writes, and combat transition on hostile response.

external_calls.purpose: Check whether the NPC is dead to choose which bark to emit.
Emit a short text line to the examining player ('dead guardsman' or 'guardsman').
Skip fast-area reactions if the NPC is dead.
Trigger a short animation/action helper for area-entry reactions (inferred behavior).
Trigger a facing/timed action after area entry (inferred from parameters and usage).
Start scheduled/timed behavior (patrol/idle/timer) with multiple timing/control parameters.
Clear the NPC dead flag (revive/reset) when schedule's spawn result indicates the need to revive.
Spawn follow-up activation/animation after clearing dead state.
Set up dialog/interaction context (sound, facing, short preparatory action) before asking questions.
Present a list of questions/options to the player and receive the chosen string for branching.
Have the guard speak lines to the player in many dialog branches (greetings, alarms, accusations).
Move the guard to a nearby tile in some dialog branches (e.g., to reposition during interrogation or action).
Obtain the guard's facing so spawned facing/timed actions match direction.
Trigger facing/timed action using the direction returned by Npc::getDir.
Mark the NPC hostile toward the player when the dialog transitions to combat.
Set combat state on the NPC to enable attacking behavior.
Assign the player as the NPC's combat target so attacks begin.
Initiate the scripted combat/attack behavior after hostility is triggered.

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to a local counter used to choose alarm/taunt lines referencing freed Titans; affects greeting/alarm dialogue selection.

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Influences alarm/taunt dialogue selection and local conversation state.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to alarm/taunt selection similar to hydrosFree/hasHeart.

reads.name: benticCacked
reads.likely_effect: Used to choose interrogation/dialogue branches related to Bentic (accusations or knowledge checks).

reads.name: interrogEW
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine which set of interrogation questions/options to present (tracks if interrogation already occurred).

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Select among alternative answers about who/what the guard protects (Devon vs Lady Mordea).

reads.name: guardEWMet
reads.likely_effect: Choose greeting lines for first-time vs repeat meetings and affect subsequent dialog choices.

reads.name: benticMet
reads.likely_effect: Influence whether the guard accuses the player of lying about knowing Bentic; affects interrogation dialog.

reads.name: askedAboutEWRoad
reads.likely_effect: Prevent repeating the same answer about where the road leads; gates one dialog reply.

writes.name: interrogEW
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the player chooses the interrogation path to mark that interrogation has occurred and avoid repeating the same option set later.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a town guard NPC that (a) responds to being examined with a short identification bark, (b) reacts briefly when the player enters its fast trigger area by spawning short-lived animations/behaviors, (c) runs a scheduled handler that can clear a dead state (revive/reset) and spawn activation behavior, and (d) provides a multi-branch interactive dialog on Use which can interrogate the player, read world-state flags to adapt answers, move or face the NPC, and transition to combat if the player responds hostilely.
